_meta.note: Regression reference for FinalWish (digital legacy tool). Re-run each case's input against the live backend (npm run check:golden final-wish) and diff for QUALITY not exact match: arrays stay non-empty, message draft present. The parse-accounts + parse-financial cases GUARD the shared cleanJsonResponse array-aware fix (lib/claude.js): both returned HTTP 500 before because cleanJsonResponse was object-only and stripped a top-level array's [ ]. DO NOT silently reverse: (1) all 7 modes sonnet-4-6; (2) cleanJsonResponse must stay array-aware (startsWith('[') branch) — many tools share it, object callers are unaffected; (3) the encrypted-export decryptor MUST slice salt as d.slice(0,16) (was 6 — a 16-byte salt is written by encryptText; wrong slice = AES-GCM fails = passphrase-protected docs unopenable); (4) all user content in the 3 HTML generators (export doc, decryptor page, QR card) MUST be escaped via esc(); (5) backend stateless + draft only in localStorage — the fws_privacy_note states this. Frontend-only fixes not covered by golden: encryption salt slice, esc() escaping, privacy note. See audit/tool-notes/FINALWISH-NOTES.md.
_meta.rate_limit_note: Backend limits 4 req/min; check:golden runs the 3 cases sequentially and fits.

payload.text: Gmail, Chase checking, Netflix, Instagram, Coinbase with my crypto, and my iCloud
Chase checking and savings, Fidelity 401k, a Vanguard Roth IRA, mortgage with Wells Fargo, and a car loan

financials.name: Car Loan
financials.notes: Auto loan; lender not specified — worth documenting lender name and account number

payload.whatToKnow: thank you for everything, I am at peace, take care of Dad

message.draft: Mom — thank you for everything, truly. Those Sunday morning pancakes come to mind so often, and I think they always will — something about the smell, the slowness of those mornings, the way they made everything feel okay. I want you to know I'm at peace, and I need you to take care of Dad for me. I love you more than I ever said out loud. — Alex
